other_observers: Tom Schultz (artist of the Peterson Warblers guide) found and identified the bird.; None
specific_location: South side of Sherrie's Pond, just N of Headquarter' Pond and just S of HQ, Chico Basin Ranch
description: While birding with Tom Schultz and Chris Wood at HQ Pond, Tom found a male Mourning Warbler. It was similar to a male MacGillivray's Warbler (MGWA), though lacked that species' white eye arcs.  The entire head was slate gray, slightly darker around the eyes, and this gray continued onto the upper chest. The lower chest was splotchy black and slate gray with no distinct line of demarcation between solid slate gray on the upper chest and the black and gray on the lower chest. At the chest-belly interface, there was a sharp line of demarcation between the black-and-gray chest and the bright yellow belly. The vent region and the undertail coverts were the same bright yellow.  The back, rump, tail, and wings were a dark olive with a brighter green cast. The undertail coverts extended at least halfway down the tail. The bill was bicolored dark above, pink below, and the legs were pink.Behaviors: Foraging low in willows and Russian OlivesCall: None heardPlumage: Definitive
